name: property-due-diligence
description: Due diligence on a property acquisition. Extract property data, build the diligence checklist, assess risks, analyze the deal structure, and draft the LOI or follow-up communications.

config.instruction: Extract all available property data: address, type, year built, square footage, lot size, zoning, environmental reports, title status, liens, current tenants and lease terms, capital improvement history, utility costs, tax assessment, insurance history.

Build the complete due diligence checklist: title search, survey, environmental (Phase I/II), structural inspection, roof report, HVAC assessment, ADA compliance, estoppel certificates, tenant interviews, financial audit (3 years), insurance review, zoning verification, and legal review of existing agreements.

Assess acquisition risks: deferred maintenance costs, environmental liability, tenant credit quality, lease rollover concentration, market direction, regulatory/zoning risks, and title defects. Quantify potential cost exposure for each risk.

Analyze the deal structure: is the price justified by the income? What's the true cost basis after deferred maintenance? Sensitivity analysis on vacancy and rate changes. Recommend: offer price, contingencies to include, holdbacks, and escrow requirements.
Draft the follow-up communications: LOI or offer letter, additional information request to seller, and internal memo summarizing findings and recommendation for the investment committee.
